other_observers: By myself when I found it on the 12th, with Brandon Percival, Mark Peterson, and Glenn Walbek on the 18th. John Drummond, Aaron Shipe and Jeannie Mitchell saw the bird on the 17th.; I found the bird on the 12th and I assume it is the same bird being seen at the later dates but I can't be certain of that.
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch, wetland below Upper Twin Pond
description: This was a small sparrow that flew out from a wet sedge meadow, seen on two different days from the same area. When it first flew I noticed a short spiky tail, light colorful back with whitis verticle stripes. It landed and perced momentarily about 1.5 feet above the veg. End of 1st sighting.  A few days later the bird was flushed into a dead Russian olive surrounded by marsh sedges. The face pattern was distinctive dull orange surrounding a light gray auricular patch. Behind the auricular was a darker anchor-shaped (widest to the rear) streak. The crown was alternating orangish and dark streaks. The breast was light warm orangish with darker flank streaks. The tail was light gray, short and with spiky points. The back was quite colorful and it was here and the auriculars that looked ratty with both juvenile and adult feathers (from photos). The back had alternating rows of dark and whitish very broad stripes, the dark feathers with some white egdes. the legs were pinkish. The bill was substantial and pinkish gray.Behaviors: Skulker, with short very low flights. When perched remained stationary until four of us moved slightly closer and then it moved about slowly in the same tree and eventually disappeared into taller bullrushes nearby.Call: none heardPlumage: suspended pre-formative
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
